title: Maxillaria stapelioides
intro: Maxillaria stapelioides is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, characterized by its unique appearance and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of wounds, infections, and other health issues. Bioactive constituents of Maxillaria stapelioides include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Maxillaria stapelioides is often prepared into tinctures, infusions, or ointments to harness its potential health benefits.
taxonomy: Maxillaria stapelioides is classified as a species within the genus Maxillaria, which is a group of orchids. It belongs to the subtribe Maxillariinae, under the tribe Maxillarieae. This plant is further classified as part of the family Orchidaceae. The subfamily Maxillarioideae and tribe Maxillarieae both belong to the subfamily Orchidoideae and are part of the monsoon orchid subclade. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Maxillaria stapelioides.

The plant's extracts have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever and respiratory issues.
